title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'We are looking for articles about sport and exercise. How has sport changed your life? Is regular exercise important for young people today? Share your views!'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Sport: A Game Changer for Mind and Body

Growing up, I was never particularly sporty. Then, at the age of fifteen, a friend persuaded me to try indoor climbing, and everything changed. Not only did I discover a passion, but I also gained confidence I never knew I had.

Regular exercise does far more than keep us physically fit. Studies consistently show that sport reduces stress, improves concentration, and boosts mental wellbeing. For young people especially, these benefits are enormous. Students who exercise regularly tend to perform better academically and handle pressure more effectively.

Unfortunately, many teenagers today lead increasingly sedentary lifestyles, spending hours in front of screens. Schools need to make sport more accessible and enjoyable, rather than focusing solely on competition. Not everyone wants to win a medal — sometimes people just want to feel good.

My advice? Find a physical activity you genuinely enjoy and make time for it. The benefits will surprise you.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You read an article in your local newspaper saying that the town council plans to close the only public sports centre due to budget cuts. Write a letter to the editor expressing your opinion about this decision and suggesting alternatives.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Editor,

I am writing in response to the article published last week regarding the proposed closure of the Westfield Sports Centre. I am deeply concerned about this decision and believe it would have a seriously negative impact on our community.

The sports centre is used by hundreds of local residents every week, from young children attending swimming lessons to elderly people participating in low-impact exercise classes. Closing this facility would remove an affordable, accessible option for people who cannot afford private gyms.

Instead of closing the centre entirely, I would urge the council to consider alternative solutions. For example, increasing membership fees slightly, seeking private sponsorship from local businesses, or applying for government sports funding could all help address the financial shortfall. Additionally, partnering with local schools to share costs might prove mutually beneficial.

Physical activity is essential for community health and wellbeing. I sincerely hope the council will reconsider this short-sighted proposal.

Yours faithfully,
A Concerned Resident

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
Your school principal has asked you to write a report on students' participation in sport and exercise at your school. Include information about current levels of participation, reasons why some students do not exercise regularly, and recommendations for improvement.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Student Sport and Exercise Participation

Introduction
This report examines current levels of physical activity among students at Hillside College and suggests ways to improve participation.

Current Participation Levels
A recent survey of 200 students revealed that only 45% exercise regularly outside of compulsory PE lessons. The most popular activities were football, swimming, and cycling. Worryingly, 30% of students reported doing no physical activity whatsoever in their free time.

Reasons for Low Participation
Students cited several barriers to exercise, including lack of time due to academic workload, limited access to affordable facilities, and a perceived lack of sporting ability. Several students also mentioned feeling self-conscious in group settings.

Recommendations
Based on these findings, the following improvements are recommended: introducing a wider variety of non-competitive activities such as yoga and dance; creating a student sports committee to organise lunchtime activities; and negotiating discounted rates at the local leisure centre.

Conclusion
With targeted investment and encouragement, student participation in sport could be significantly increased.
